title: "I Located A UFO Base In Arizona!" (Ft. Ross Coulthart)
summary: Ross Coulthart discusses his investigative journey into UFOs, from early skepticism to confirmation through high-level sources including former Navy scientist Nat Kobitz. The interview covers the Bogota sphere phenomenon, Australian UFO history including the 1978 Kaikoura incident, and claims about Lockheed Martin holding alien spacecraft that the CIA prevented from being transferred to Bigelow Aerospace.

acronyms.definition: The equivalent of the DIA (Defense Intelligence Agency) in Australia at the time

events.description: Mario Woods had an experience at Ellsworth Air Force Base
Kaikoura UFO incident in New Zealand where cargo aircraft pilots witnessed metallic disc-shaped objects confirmed by radar
Release of New Zealand archives revealing Air Force cover-up of Kaikoura incident
New York Times published Pentagon UFO story
Ross Coulthart began writing his book and was told by sources in Georgetown pub that 'it's all about consciousness'
Ross Coulthart ran the Jim Marron sphere story on Channel 7 Australia, which was viewed over 43 million times
NASA UAP review panel conducted with David Spergel

key_claims.claim: The United States has recovered multiple alien craft including from Roswell, Aztec, and Kingman incidents
Lockheed Martin was going to pass control of an alien spacecraft to Bigelow Aerospace but the CIA intervened to stop it
A Lockheed Martin senior executive implicitly acknowledged they are holding alien technology and said 'it's the agency' preventing disclosure
Nat Kobitz was shown a piece of bulkhead at Wright Patterson AFB that was bonded at the atomic level, atom by atom layering
The metamaterials story in the New York Times was 'always nonsense' - it was actually about a spacecraft
Former New Zealand Prime Minister Robert Muldoon said 'the Americans wanted that one shut down pretty quickly' regarding the 1978 Kaikoura UFO incident
People have been murdered in connection with UAP secrecy based on FBI briefing given to Congress
Americans have been secretly testing hypersonic missile technology at Woomera range in Australia
The Bogota sphere contains an aluminum alloy three times the hardness of conventional aerospace materials
Jim Marron's sphere was witnessed moving under its own power on a level floor with no visible propulsion
